content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 1, 2003


Jennifer Tierney
Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy
7290 Navajo Road, Suite 207
San Diego, CA 92119

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-077

Dear Ms. Tierney:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy, a controlled committee, regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. May Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy contract with Drew Development, LLC, for political services if the candidate's adult child is an employee of Drew Development?

2. Is the answer to the first question affected if the adult child is assigned duties related to the Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy campaign?

CONCLUSIONS

1. There is nothing in the Act that would prohibit Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy from contracting with Drew Development, LLC, as long as the payments are for services that have a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

2. The conclusion does not change if the candidate's adult child working for Drew Development, LLC, is assigned duties related to the Friends of Mayor Dick Murphy campaign.

_______________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-03-077
Page No. 2

FACTS

Drew Development is a limited liability company that provides political
consulting and fundraising services to various political candidates. Friends of Mayor
Dick Murphy, a committee controlled by Dick Murphy, wishes to retain the company's
services. Drew Development is considering hiring Mayor Murphy's adult child (age 24
and not a dependent) as an employee of the company.

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act's provisions concerning the permissible uses of
campaign funds are found in sections 89510-89522, copy enclosed. Under these
provisions, campaign funds are regarded as totally separate and distinct from a candidate
or officeholder's personal funds. The gist of the Act's campaign funds provisions is to
ensure that campaign funds are used for electioneering and officeholder purposes, and are
not expended for the candidate or officeholder's personal purposes.

At the outset, the Act provides that all contributions deposited into a campaign
account are "held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the candidate or for
expenses associated with holding office." (Section 89510.) Generally speaking, an
expenditure related to seeking or holding office is permissible under the Act if it is
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.² (Section 89512.)
Certain specified uses of campaign funds, including those that are considered to confer a
"substantial personal benefit"³ on the candidate must meet a higher standard and be
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.)

Although we conclude in the footnote below that payment by your committee to
Drew Development, LLC, for its political consulting and fundraising services does not
confer a "substantial personal benefit" on Mayor Murphy, the payment for professional
services must still meet the higher standard of being directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose under section 89513(b). Section 89513(b) provides
as follows:

² Please note, if funds used are surplus campaign funds, additional restrictions may apply.
³ "Substantial personal benefit" is defined to mean "an expenditure of campaign funds which
results in a direct personal benefit with a value of more than two hundred dollars ($200) to a candidate,
elected officer, or any individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign
funds held by a committee" (§ 89511(b)(3).) Moreover, regulation 18960 provides that an expenditure of
campaign funds results in a "direct personal benefit" when a member of the candidate or elected official's
immediate family realizes an increase in his or her income of more than $200 from the expenditure. (Reg.
18960(a)(1).) Section 82029 of the Act defines "immediate family" to mean an official's spouse and
dependent children. A child is considered a "dependent child" for purposes of the Act if the child is under
18 years of age and the elected official is entitled to claim the dependent child as such for income tax
purposes. (Tremlett Advice Letter, No. I-89-386.) Mayor Murphy's child is over the age of 18 and,
therefore, is not considered a member of the candidate's "immediate family" under this definition.
Consequently, cash payments of $200 or more made to Drew Development, LLC, to compensate for
political consulting and fundraising services are not considered to confer a direct personal benefit on Mayor
Murphy, even if his child is assigned duties related to Mayor Murphy's committee.

File No. A-03-077
Page No. 3

"(b) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay for or reimburse the cost of professional services unless the services are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(1) Expenditures by a committee to pay for professional services reasonably required by the committee to assist it in the performance of its administrative functions are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose."

We have advised that the compensation must be comparable to the services performed or a gift may result. (Harden Advice Letter, No. A-90-498.) Under section 89513(b), so long as Drew Development, LLC, performs duties that are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose, and the company is paid fair market value for those services, the payment for professional services by the committee will be permissible.⁴

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division

KSM:jg
I:\AdviceLetrs\03-077

___________________

⁴ Please note that campaign funds may not be used to compensate Mayor Murphy for the performance of similar duties for his own campaign committee. (Section 89518.)
